what_it_does: It is a self-described factual repo snapshot taken before a context compaction: it records the derived substrate, design knowledge, tokens, render engine, build tools, surfaces, live servers, pieces, cognition fabric, the author's own read/edit state, a complete mechanical inventory of tracked files, and the git-ignored reference/source directories that contain the design foundation.
observations: The file opens with a bias warning that sections 1–11 are selection-biased and miss whole areas such as factory/, probes/, archive/, content/, assets/, state/, PROJECT.md, and FORMATION_JOURNAL.md.
It states the authoritative whole-repo record is the 643-file mechanical inventory plus registry/address-registry.json, not the curated slice.
scan.py structurally excludes source/ and reference/, which are git-ignored and contain the real design-system CSS, QA screens, brand assets, and deep docs.
The registry/address-registry.json is noted as stale as of Jun 18 and needs a re-run of engine/substrate tools.
It records specific edits made this session, including warmed tokens, a covers branch in designLibraryObject, and a currently non-working two-panel render in archetype.js.
It lists live server ports and which are phone-reachable versus localhost-only.
It documents the cognition substrate/fabric channels and the recall chunk schema with pending design fields.
It ends with the untracked/uncommitted files from git status.
standouts: The central tension between a curated, partial read and a complete mechanical inventory is explicitly flagged rather than hidden.
It warns that any registry-based dragnet misses the design foundation because source/ and reference/ are ignored by scan.py and by git.
It records the author's own knowledge limits, unverified edits, and broken code state as facts rather than passing judgment.
conventions: Follows a structured markdown report with numbered sections and a final mechanical file inventory.
Uses self-correction and epistemic-uncertainty markers such as '[I have NOT read this]' and '⚠️ BIAS WARNING'.
Breaks the convention of presenting a curated summary as complete by delegating authority to the mechanical inventory.
Separates opinion-bearing material to a different file (DNA-TRANSFER-PACK.md).
concerns: The derived registry is stale and the proper refresh pipeline is noted but not confirmed as run.
Several edits are marked as unverified-by-render, including token color changes and the archetype.js two-panel visual.
A non-working render state is left in the codebase and documented here rather than fixed.
A palette discrepancy between the spec gold and tokens.json gold is recorded but not reconciled.
The file itself is a snapshot that may become outdated as soon as files are added, moved, or deleted.
notes: A self-aware factual baseline that contrasts a partial curated read with a full mechanical inventory and flags the git-ignored design-system directories as essential but invisible.
questions: What exactly is the 'context compaction' this file is meant to precede?
How are the 11 deep extraction JSONs structured and what fields do they share?
What is the intended refresh cadence for registry/address-registry.json and who triggers it?
How should a dragnet or build process incorporate source/ and reference/ given that scan.py excludes them?
What is the planned resolution for the recorded gold-color discrepancy between the spec and tokens.json?
What is the intended fix for the currently non-working two-panel render in archetype.js?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is a factual record with no appraisals, and the content is largely a mechanical inventory plus transparent notes about what the author has read, edited, and left broken.
apparent_themes: Registry-as-truth with explicit caveats about staleness and exclusions
intention_signals: It appears designed to create an audit-friendly baseline that future reasoning can use without over-trusting the author's curated summary, by explicitly mapping known-knowns, known-unknowns, and ignored-but-critical material.
novelty: It is a repo record that systematically documents its own blind spots and warns readers not to treat its curated sections as the whole repo, while also surfacing the git-ignored directories that actually hold the design foundation.
contribution: It contributes a bias-aware, mechanical file inventory, an explicit map of ignored-but-essential design-system directories, and a transparent point-in-time account of the author's read state and edits.
summary_for_embedding: DNA-REPO-FACTS.md is a self-described factual pre-compaction record of the counterpart-design repository, listing the derived substrate, design knowledge, tokens, render engine, build tools, surfaces, servers, pieces, cognition fabric, author's read/edit state, a complete 643-file mechanical inventory, and the git-ignored reference/source design-system directories.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to establish an objective, bias-aware baseline snapshot that separates recorded facts from opinions and warns future readers not to rely on its curated sections as the whole repo.
